# Administrator Roles and Responsibilities

## Overview

Each department, chair, and organizational unit at TUM designates IT administrators to manage local IT infrastructure and serve as the liaison with TUM's central IT services (IT Service Center and LRZ). These administrators are the primary contact points for IT-related matters within their organizational units.

## Administrator Types

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Administrator Hierarchy"
flowchart TB
    LI["Department / Chair Head"]
    LI --> |appoints| Admin["IT Administrator<br/>(EDV-Betreuer)"]
    LI --> |appoints| TUMCoord["TUMonline Coordinator<br/>(TUMonline-Beauftragte)"]

    Admin --> |manages| IT["Local IT Infrastructure"]
    TUMCoord --> |manages| Users["User & Group Administration"]

    subgraph Central["Central IT Partners"]
        ITSZ["IT Service Center (ITSZ)"]
        LRZ["LRZ"]
    end

    Admin --> |coordinates with| Central
    TUMCoord --> |coordinates with| Central
```

### IT Administrator (EDV-Betreuer)

The **IT Administrator** is the primary technical contact for IT matters:

| Aspect | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Appointment** | Designated by department/chair head |
| **Scope** | Technical infrastructure and systems management |
| **Authority** | Access to TUMonline admin functions and LRZ services |
| **Primary Role** | Day-to-day IT operations and user support |

### TUMonline Coordinator (TUMonline-Beauftragte)

The **TUMonline Coordinator** manages users and groups through TUM's central platform:

| Aspect | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Appointment** | Designated by department/chair head |
| **Scope** | User account and group management via TUMonline |
| **Authority** | Access to TUMonline keyuser areas |
| **Primary Role** | User lifecycle management and access control |

## Key Responsibilities

### 1. User Management

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "User Management Workflow"
flowchart LR
    Request["User Request"]
    Request --> Admin["Administrator<br/>Review"]
    Admin --> TUMonline["TUMonline<br/>Processing"]
    TUMonline --> LDAP["LDAP / AD<br/>Synchronization"]
    LDAP --> Access["User<br/>Access Granted"]
```

Administrators are responsible for:

- **User Accounts**: Managing user accounts via TUMonline
- **Group Management**: Creating and managing user groups
- **Access Control**: Defining and maintaining access permissions
- **Account Lifecycle**: Managing account creation, modification, and termination

### 2. Infrastructure Management

Administrators manage departmental infrastructure including:

- **Workstations**: Laptops, desktops, and specialized equipment
- **Printers**: Network printers and printing resources
- **Servers**: Virtual machines via LRZ and hosted hardware
- **Network Equipment**: Switches, access points, and network configuration

### 3. Service Configuration

Configuration responsibilities include:

- **Network Services**: Coordinating DNS, DHCP, and VLAN configuration with IT Service Center
- **VPN Access**: Setting up remote access for users via MWN VPN
- **Email Services**: Managing mailing lists and shared mailboxes via TUMonline
- **Storage**: Coordinating NAS access with LRZ

### 4. Support Coordination

Administrators serve as liaison between users and central IT:

- **First-Level Support**: Initial troubleshooting and issue resolution
- **Escalation**: Forwarding complex issues to IT Service Center (it-support@tum.de)
- **Communication**: Relaying IT announcements and updates
- **Training**: Orienting users to available IT services

## Onboarding New Administrators

### Getting Started

New administrators should:

1. **Contact IT Service Center**: Schedule an introduction (it-support@tum.de)
2. **Access TUMonline**: Obtain admin permissions and familiarize with the interface
3. **Review Documentation**: Study the TUM Wiki and LRZ documentation
4. **Meet Predecessors**: If possible, coordinate handover with previous administrators

### Consulting Services

The IT Service Center offers consulting for new administrators:

::: {.callout-note}
## Consulting Appointment
The IT Service Center gladly advises new IT administrators on:

- Infrastructure questions
- System integration requirements
- Introduction to TUM IT services and tools

**Contact**: it-support@tum.de to schedule an appointment
:::

## Best Practices

### Documentation

- Maintain records of all infrastructure and configurations
- Document custom configurations and non-standard setups
- Keep user access records up to date

### Communication

- Respond promptly to user requests
- Communicate scheduled maintenance windows
- Report security incidents immediately

### Security

- Regularly review user access permissions
- Ensure proper account termination procedures
- Follow TUM security guidelines

### Continuity

- Establish backup administrators for coverage
- Document procedures for handover
- Maintain up-to-date contact information with IT Service Center

## Administrative Tools Access

| Tool | Purpose | Access Method |
|------|---------|---------------|
| **TUMonline** | User/group management | [campus.tum.de](https://campus.tum.de) |
| **LRZ Documentation** | Service guides | [doku.lrz.de](https://doku.lrz.de) |
| **TUM Wiki** | Documentation | [wiki.tum.de](https://wiki.tum.de) |
| **IT Service Center** | Support requests | it-support@tum.de |
